mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable model serving, with support for various hardware configurations. Key trade-offs include the need for GPU resources for optimal performance and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Add Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the vLLM Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.17.0

```

> This command adds the vLLM repository for version 0.17.0.

steps.title: Install Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
Install the necessary CRDs for vLLM to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/main/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml

```

> This command installs the CRDs required for vLLM.

steps.title: Install vLLM using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the vLLM application in the specified namespace.

```bash

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.17.0

```

> This command installs vLLM in the 'vllm' namespace.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource (CR)
steps.description:
Create a sample vLLM instance to verify the installation.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-2'
  replicas: 1
EOF

```

> This command creates an example vLLM instance using the GPT-2 model.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-2'
  replicas: 1
EOF

steps.title: Verify the vLLM instance
steps.description:
Check the status of the vLLM instance to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get vllms -n vllm

```

> This command lists all vLLM instances in the 'vllm' namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This step will remove the vLLM installation and associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

kubectl delete namespace vllm

```

> This command uninstalls the vLLM release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any necessary data. This process includes draining the pods, upgrading, and verifying the installation.

```bash

kubectl drain <pod-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.17.0

kubectl get vllms -n vllm

```

> Replace <pod-name> with the name of the vLLM pod to drain.
> This command upgrades vLLM to the specified version.
> Verify that the vLLM instance is running correctly after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'vllm not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM Helm chart may not be installed or the repository is not added.

**Fix:**
Run 'helm repo update' and ensure the vLLM repository is added correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create VLLM instance'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The CRD for vLLM may not be installed properly.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRD using 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/main/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml'.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM instance may be misconfigured or the model is not available.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the vLLM pods.

**Fix:**
Ensure your cluster has sufficient resources or adjust the resource requests in the vLLM configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new models and improved memory management features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the vLLM instance listed with 'kubectl get vllms -n vllm', showing the status as 'Running'.

EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources.
